mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerates, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive SPX returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDPC1 growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets and rising wage growth, which historically precedes equity rallies as consumer spending strengthens.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not outperform the unconditional mean when UNRATE crosses below 5.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in core inflation and the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate faster than nominal earnings can adjust, yielding negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surges during late-cycle expansions indicate robust manufacturing demand, providing a short-term momentum boost to cyclical equities over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises >1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, leading to negative SPX returns over the following month as financial sector earnings deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where HOUST crosses below 550 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with steady employment growth confirm durable consumer demand, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when RSAFS and PAYEMS both show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Higher policy rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities and raise borrowing costs for leveraged firms, systematically depressing short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annually while inflation remains below 2.0%, the economy achieves a Goldilocks state that maximizes equity risk premiums and generates positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when GDPC1 annualized growth >2.5% and CPIAUCSL annualized inflation <2.0%.
